bindings.quest_focus: the road where my brother was killed
bindings.quest_stakes: a family's honor and a brother's memory
bindings.quest_threat: the creatures who left him in a ditch like refuse

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}. Come closer. I need to speak quietly, because what I'm about to ask carries weight that most people can't bear.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My brother was killed by {enemy_type} three weeks ago on the road {location_hint}. He was bringing {quest_item} back for the harvest festival. {quest_threat} left his body in the ditch. The guards say they can't pursue because it happened outside their jurisdiction. Jurisdiction. {target_npc} helped me bury him, and they're the only one who understands that I can't let this stand.
dialogueChunks.outro: I'm not asking you to feel what I feel. I'm asking you to {quest_action} where I cannot. {quest_stakes} demand it.

playerResponses.text: Your brother will be avenged. Tell me everything about the ones who did this.
Family blood demands an answer. I'll see it done, for a fair price.
I'm sorry for your loss, but vengeance isn't a path I walk.

bindings.quest_action: destroy the raiders who burned our family's home twice
bindings.quest_stakes: a family that has bled enough for one lifetime
bindings.quest_threat: the raiders who keep coming back to finish what they started
bindings.quest_origin: the first burning two months ago

dialogueChunks.intro: You don't know me, {player_name}, but after today, you'll know my family's name. Everyone here does.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} burned {quest_focus} two months ago. My mother, my aunt, three cousins: all gone. We rebuilt, and then {quest_threat} came back and did it again. Took the {quest_item} we'd stockpiled, scattered our livestock {location_hint}. {target_npc} took in the survivors, but {quest_stakes} are broken and angry. The elders say to endure. I say the time for endurance is over.

playerResponses.text: Twice they struck your family? There won't be a third time. I swear it.
I understand the fury. Point me in the right direction.
I grieve for your family, but this kind of fight draws more blood, not less.

bindings.quest_action: carry out the community's solemn vow of vengeance
bindings.quest_focus: the gathered intelligence on enemy positions
bindings.quest_stakes: thirty families who each have a name to mourn
bindings.quest_threat: the band that has been killing our people for months

dialogueChunks.intro: We held a gathering last night, {player_name}. Every family who's lost someone to the {enemy_type}. There were thirty of us.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_stakes}, each with a grief to carry. My own father was among the first, six months ago. He went to deliver {quest_item} to a trading post {location_hint} and never came back. {target_npc} organized the gathering because they lost their sister to {quest_threat}. We've pooled our resources and knowledge. We know where they roost and their patrol routes.
dialogueChunks.outro: This isn't one family's grief anymore. This is a community's solemn vow. Will you {quest_action}?

playerResponses.text: Thirty families. Thirty reasons. I'll carry your vow to its end.
With that much intel, I can make this work. Show me the maps.
A community's vengeance is a heavy burden. I can't carry it.

bindings.quest_action: make the creatures who took my daughter know her life had weight
bindings.quest_focus: the path where my daughter was taken
bindings.quest_stakes: a healer's memory and the community she served
bindings.quest_threat: the creatures that killed a woman who only ever helped others
bindings.quest_origin: a healing errand that ended in tragedy

dialogueChunks.intro: I've been sitting at this table since dawn, {player_name}, trying to find words for something that words can't reach.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My daughter was a healer. She went {location_hint} to gather {quest_item} for a sick child, because that's who she was. {quest_threat} found her before she made it back. {target_npc} found what was left the next morning. The whole settlement turned out for the burial. Half of them brought weapons: farmers and shopkeepers who want to march on the enemy camp. They'd be slaughtered.
dialogueChunks.outro: I don't want more death. But I need you to {quest_action}. {quest_stakes} demand nothing less.

playerResponses.text: Her life mattered. And the ones who ended it will answer for that.
A healer, killed while helping others. I'll make sure it wasn't for nothing.
Nothing I do will bring her back. I'm sorry.
